source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for VPC Network Changes

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for VPC Network Changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) network changes.
input_row.rationale: It is possible to have more than one VPC within a project. In addition, it is also possible to create a peer connection between two VPCs enabling network traffic to route between VPCs. Monitoring changes to a VPC will help ensure VPC traffic flow is not getting impacted. Impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Ensure the prescribed log metric is present: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure at least one metric <Log_Metric_Name> is present with filter text: resource.type="gce_network" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.insert" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.patch" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.removePeering" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.addPeering") Ensure the prescribed alerting policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name> stream is above a threshold of 0 for greater than 0 seconds means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alerting thresholds make sense for the user's organization. 5. Ensure that appropriate notification channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure the log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with filter set to: resource.type="gce_network" AND protoPayload.methodName="beta.compute.networks.insert" OR protoPayload.methodName="beta.compute.networks.patch" OR protoPayload.methodName="v1.compute.networks.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName="v1.compute.networks.removePeering" OR protoPayload.methodName="v1.compute.networks.addPeering" 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>. Ensure the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains at least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>\" • AND enabled is set to true
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Create the prescribed log metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter. 3. Clear any text and add: resource.type="gce_network" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.insert" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.patch" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.removePeering" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.addPeering") 4. Click Submit Filter. Display logs appear based on the filter text entered by the user. 5. In the Metric Editor menu on the right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and Type to Counter. This ensures that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the user's advanced logs query. 6. Click Create Metric. Create the prescribed alert policy: 1. Identify the newly created metric under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the new metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page appears. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the user's organization. For example, a threshold of 0 for the most recent value will ensure that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value 4. Configure the desired notification channels in the section Notifications. 5. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create the prescribed Log Metric: • Use the command: gcloud logging metrics create Create the prescribed alert policy: • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/logs-based-metrics/ 2. https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/custom-metrics/ 3. https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerts/ 4. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/tools/gcloud-logging 5. https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/overview
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control "Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for VPC Network Changes" can be fully verified using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI commands. Here's the rationale for choosing an automated approach:

- **Can GCP APIs query this property?** Yes, GCP APIs allow querying of log-based metrics and monitoring alert policies.
- **Is this a standard GCP service property (encryption, logging, access control, network rules)?** Yes, it deals with logging and monitoring, which are standard GCP service properties.
- **Does Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK have methods to check this?** Yes, `gcloud logging metrics list` and `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` are directly mentioned in the audit section.
- **Does GCP CLI have commands to retrieve this?** Yes, the audit section provides specific commands to list and verify log metrics and alert policies.
- **Is it about IAM, S3, EC2, VPC, CloudTrail, Config, KMS, etc.?** Yes, it concerns VPC network changes and corresponding log alerts.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_vpc_network_changes_alert_existing

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

The automated program would perform the following checks:

- **GCP API Calls:**
  - Use `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` to list log metrics.
  - Verify that the returned JSON includes a metric with the filter: 
    ```
    resource.type="gce_network" AND 
    (protoPayload.methodName="compute.networks.insert" 
    OR protoPayload.methodName="compute.networks.patch" 
    OR protoPayload.methodName="compute.networks.delete" 
    OR protoPayload.methodName="compute.networks.removePeering" 
    OR protoPayload.methodName="compute.networks.addPeering")
    ```

- **Check for Alert Policies:**
  - Use `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json` to list alerting policies.
  - Verify that there is at least one alert policy where the condition `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` is set to:
    ```
    metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>"
    ```

- **Enumeration of Resources:**
  - Enumerate all existing log metrics and alert policies within the project to ensure complete checks against each.

- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - The existence of a specific log-based metric with the correct filter for VPC changes.
  - The presence of an alert policy linked to the log metric with proper conditions and thresholds set (threshold > 0).

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** A log metric with the appropriate filter and a corresponding alert policy exists with the correct conditions.
  - **Fail:** Missing log metrics or alert policies, or if the configurations are incorrect.

This approach ensures comprehensive coverage using automated checks via GCP tools, aligning with industry standards for CSPM tools.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated:** The classification as "Automated" is correct. Both the existence of log-based metrics and alert policies can be fully queried and validated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud` CLI).
- **GCP SDK/CLI Capability:** The `gcloud logging metrics list` and `gcloud monitoring policies list` (preferably GA or Beta, not Alpha) commands can enumerate the required resources and their configuration. This is a standard approach for CSPM tooling and compliance automation.
- **Other GCP Tools:** There is no need for manual review unless the configuration is highly customized or non-standard. No CloudFormation (AWS-only) or GCP Config (Config Validator) is strictly required for this check, though they could be used for policy-as-code.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **8/10**

- **Automation Details:** The steps to use `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` and `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json` are technically correct for enumerating log metrics and alerting policies.
- **gcloud Commands:** 
  - `gcloud logging metrics list` is correct and GA.
  - For alerting policies, `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` is technically valid but **should use** `gcloud monitoring policies list` (GA) or `gcloud beta monitoring policies list` for production automation, as alpha commands are not recommended for compliance automation due to instability.
- **Metric Filter:** The metric filter string is accurate and matches the CIS recommendation.
- **Alert Policy Validation:** The check for `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` is correct, but the logic should also ensure the alert is **enabled** and has a valid notification channel (email, SMS, webhook, etc.), which is a CIS best practice.
- **Program Name:** `gcp_logging_vpc_network_changes_alert_existing` is descriptive, but could be improved for clarity and specificity (see below).

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Use GA/Beta gcloud Commands:** Replace `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` with `gcloud monitoring policies list` or `gcloud beta monitoring policies list` to ensure stability and future compatibility.
- **Check Notification Channel:** The automation should also verify that the alert policy has at least one notification channel configured, as per CIS intent (alerting, not just metric existence).
- **Check Alert Policy Status:** Ensure the alert policy is **enabled** and not in a disabled/snoozed state.
- **Check Metric Scope:** Confirm that the log metric is scoped to the correct project and is not a legacy metric or misconfigured.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** For clarity and convention, use:  
  `gcp_logging_vpc_network_changes_alert_configured`  
  or  
  `gcp_logging_logmetric_alert_vpc_network_changes`
- **Alternative API Methods:** For deeper automation, consider using the REST API (`projects.metrics.list`, `projects.alertPolicies.list`) for more granular control and integration into custom tooling.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The approach is sound, and the technical details are mostly correct. Minor improvements are needed for completeness and best practice alignment.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

- **Modify** the automation to:
  - Use GA or Beta `gcloud` commands for alert policy enumeration.
  - Explicitly check for notification channels and alert policy status.
  - Consider improving the program name for clarity.
  - Optionally, document the REST API endpoints for future extensibility.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section              | Assessment                                                                                 |
|----------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation  | AGREE                                                                                     |
| Technical Accuracy   | 8/10                                                                                      |
| Improvements         | Use GA/Beta gcloud, check notification channels, alert status, improve program name        |
| Confidence           | HIGH                                                                                      |
| Final Recommendation | MODIFY (as above)                                                                         |

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the existence and configuration of log-based metrics and alerting policies for VPC network changes can be fully verified using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). The required properties (log metric filters and alert policies) are directly accessible and enumerable via standard, stable CLI commands (`gcloud logging metrics list` and `gcloud monitoring policies list`). There is no need for manual inspection, as all compliance criteria are programmatically auditable.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_logging_vpc_network_change_metric_and_alert_exists

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Logging (Log-based Metrics), Monitoring (Alert Policies)
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - `gcloud logging metrics list --format=json`  
  - `gcloud monitoring policies list --format=json`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - For log-based metrics: Presence of a metric with a filter matching  
    ```
    resource.type="gce_network" AND (
      protoPayload.methodName="compute.networks.insert" OR
      protoPayload.methodName="compute.networks.patch" OR
      protoPayload.methodName="compute.networks.delete" OR
      protoPayload.methodName="compute.networks.addPeering" OR
      protoPayload.methodName="compute.networks.removePeering"
    )
    ```
  - For alert policies: Existence of an alert policy that references the above log-based metric.
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - At least one log-based metric exists with the specified filter.
  - At least one alerting policy exists that triggers on this metric.
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - No log-based metric with the specified filter exists, or
  - No alerting policy is configured to alert on this metric.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on the automated approach. The only minor technical note was to use the GA (`gcloud monitoring policies list`) rather than the alpha command for alerting policies, which is reflected in the final automation details.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
